devanagari: चाणूरमुष्टिकादीनां मल्लानां निधनं कंसस्य वधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं चर्चितसङ्कल्पो भगवान् मधुसूदनः । आससादाथ चणूरं मुष्टिकं रोहिणीसुतः ...
english_devnagari: cāṇūramuṣṭikādīnāṃ mallānāṃ nidhanaṃ kaṃsasya vadhaścaśrīśuka uvācaevaṃ carcitasaṅkalpo bhagavān madhusūdanaḥāsasādātha caṇūraṃ muṣṭikaṃ rohiṇīsutaḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka continued:
(When the pairs (or wrestlers) were so determined) Lord Kṛṣṇa (the slayer of demon Madhu) of unflinching resolution caught hold of Cāṇūra and Balarāma, the son of Rohiṇī advanced towards Muṣṭika.
1(A).[1] (Spurious—vide the foot-note)
purport:
Footnote [1]:
D.S. Yande’s edition of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa Bombay 1928 adds this spurious verse. As Vṛndāvana edition does not give it, I did not include it above:
āsphoṭya hastau hastābhyām ūrū caiva punaḥ puṇaḥ /
visṛjya bhūyaḥ saṅgṛhya parikramya parasparam //
It describes how the wrestlers stroked loudly their arms and thighs, closed upon each other and tried to dodge.
I regard it as ‘spurious’ as some standard annotators such as Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā, Padaratnāvalī, Subodhinī, VT. do not take its cognisance.

devanagari: हस्ताभ्यां हस्तयोर्बद्ध्वा पद्‍भ्यामेव च पादयोः । विचकर्षतुरन्योन्यं प्रसह्य विजिगीषया ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: hastābhyāṃ hastayorbaddhvā pad‍bhyāmeva ca pādayoḥvicakarṣaturanyonyaṃ prasahya vijigīṣayā
translation: With the ambition of vanquishing the opponent, they interlocked their hands and feet with those of the opponent and vehemently tugged each other (with all their strength).

devanagari: अरत्‍नी द्वे अरत्‍निभ्यां जानुभ्यां चैव जानुनी । शिरः शीर्ष्णोरसोरस्तौ अन्योन्यं अभिजघ्नतुः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: arat‍nī dve arat‍nibhyāṃ jānubhyāṃ caiva jānunīśiraḥ śīrṣṇorasorastau anyonyaṃ abhijaghnatuḥ
translation: While wrestling they struck each other with fore-arms with clenched fists[2], knees with knees, heads with heads and chests with chests.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
tasmān muṣṭyupa-lakṣitatvena kathañcit muṣṭir eva vācyā—VT

devanagari: परिभ्रामणविक्षेप परिरम्भावपातनैः । उत्सर्पणापसर्पणैः चान्योन्यं प्रत्यरुन्धताम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: paribhrāmaṇavikṣepa parirambhāvapātanaiḥutsarpaṇāpasarpaṇaiḥ cānyonyaṃ pratyarundhatām
translation: They mutually tried to overpower each other by whirling, squeezing into arms, dashing each other to the ground and by pouncing on each other or dodging by receding.

devanagari: उत्थापनैरुन्नयनैः चालनैः स्थापनैरपि । परस्परं जिगीषन्तौ अपचक्रतुरात्मनः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: utthāpanairunnayanaiḥ cālanaiḥ sthāpanairapiparasparaṃ jigīṣantau apacakraturātmanaḥ
translation: Fired with the ambition to vanquish the other, they subjected each other’s bodies to sufferings by lifting up, by carrying away, dashing down on the ground and fixing the opponent to the spot.

devanagari: तद्‍बलाबलवद् युद्धं समेताः सर्वयोषितः । ऊचुः परस्परं राजन् सानुकम्पा वरूथशः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tad‍balābalavad yuddhaṃ sametāḥ sarvayoṣitaḥūcuḥ parasparaṃ rājan sānukampā varūthaśaḥ
translation: Noticing that unequally-arranged fight between the weak boys and the powerful athletes, all the lady-spectators, being full of compassion, spoke (criticising) in groups, to each other, Oh king!

devanagari: महानयं बताधर्म एषां राजसभासदाम् । ये बलाबलवद् युद्धं राज्ञोऽन्विच्छन्ति पश्यतः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: mahānayaṃ batādharma eṣāṃ rājasabhāsadāmye balābalavad yuddhaṃ rājño'nvicchanti paśyataḥ
translation: “Alas! It is out and out injustice on the part of the royal assembly who revel in such unequal fighting between the weak and the strong opponents, despite the presence of the king as spectator.

devanagari: क्व वज्रसारसर्वाङ्गौ मल्लौ शैलेन्द्रसन्निभौ । क्व चातिसुकुमाराङ्गौ किशोरौ नाप्तयौवनौ ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: kva vajrasārasarvāṅgau mallau śailendrasannibhaukva cātisukumārāṅgau kiśorau nāptayauvanau
translation: What a great contrast is there between the athletes of adamant-like powerful bodies, enormous like the hills, as opposed to the two saplings (teenagers) of very delicate limbs and who have not even attained youth.

devanagari: धर्मव्यतिक्रमो ह्यस्य समाजस्य ध्रुवं भवेत् । यत्राधर्मः समुत्तिष्ठेत् न स्थेयं तत्र कर्हिचित् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: dharmavyatikramo hyasya samājasya dhruvaṃ bhavetyatrādharmaḥ samuttiṣṭhet na stheyaṃ tatra karhicit
translation: This is certainly the transgression of righteousness on the part of this assembly. One should never stay at all at a place where violation of religion and justice takes place.

devanagari: न सभां प्रविशेत् प्राज्ञः सभ्यदोषान् अनुस्मरन् । अब्रुवन् विब्रुवन्नज्ञो नरः किल्बिषमश्नुते ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: na sabhāṃ praviśet prājñaḥ sabhyadoṣān anusmaranabruvan vibruvannajño naraḥ kilbiṣamaśnute
translation: An intelligent person who is aware of the faults of the members of attending an assembly, ought not to enter that assembly at all. Irrespective of his participation in the discussion of the assembly by remaining silent despite knowing the truth or making a non-factual statement), the attending member incurs sins.

devanagari: वल्गतः शत्रुमभितः कृष्णस्य वदनाम्बुजम् । वीक्ष्यतां श्रमवार्युप्तं पद्मकोशमिवाम्बुभिः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: valgataḥ śatrumabhitaḥ kṛṣṇasya vadanāmbujamvīkṣyatāṃ śramavāryuptaṃ padmakośamivāmbubhiḥ
translation: Lo! Have a look at the lotus-face of Kṛṣṇa. How his countenance appears adorned with droplets of perspiration and resembles a lotus covered with drops of water, as he whirls round his opponent.

devanagari: किं न पश्यत रामस्य मुखमाताम्रलोचनम् । मुष्टिकं प्रति सामर्षं हाससंरम्भशोभितम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ na paśyata rāmasya mukhamātāmralocanammuṣṭikaṃ prati sāmarṣaṃ hāsasaṃrambhaśobhitam
translation: Don’t you see the face of Balarāma, with reddish eyes, flushed with rage against Muṣṭika, yet beautified with a defiant laugh.

devanagari: पुण्या बत व्रजभुवो यदयं नृलिङ्ग । गूढः पुराणपुरुषो वनचित्रमाल्यः । गाः पालयन्सहबलः क्वणयंश्च वेणुं । विक्रीदयाञ्चति गिरित्ररमार्चिताङ्...
english_devnagari: puṇyā bata vrajabhuvo yadayaṃ nṛliṅgagūḍhaḥ purāṇapuruṣo vanacitramālyaḥgāḥ pālayansahabalaḥ kvaṇayaṃśca veṇuṃvikrīdayāñcati giritraramārcitāṅghriḥ
translation: Oh! How blessed must be the lands of Vraja where this Ancient Person whose feet are worshipped by god Śiva and goddess Lakṣmī roams about in the guise of a human being, decorated with wreaths of quaint wild flowers, playing upon his flute, tending cows in the company of Balarāma and goes out for playing games (Shame upon this assembly where he is maltreated thus).

devanagari: गोप्यस्तपः किमचरन् यदमुष्य रूपं । लावण्यसारमसमोर्ध्वमनन्यसिद्धम् । दृग्भिः पिबन्त्यनुसवाभिनवं दुरापम् । एकान्तधाम यशसः श्रीय ऐश्वरस्य ...
english_devnagari: gopyastapaḥ kimacaran yadamuṣya rūpaṃlāvaṇyasāramasamordhvamananyasiddhamdṛgbhiḥ pibantyanusavābhinavaṃ durāpamekāntadhāma yaśasaḥ śrīya aiśvarasya
translation: What supreme type of penance the Gopīs must have practised that they drink with their eyes his personal charm which is the essence of beauty, neither equalled nor surpassed by anything, which is by itself too beautiful to be adorned by anything external which assumed novelty every moment and is very difficult to obtain, as it is the permanent absolute abode of glory, beauty, affluence and almightiness.

devanagari: या दोहनेऽवहनने मथनोपलेप । प्रेङ्खेङ्खनार्भरुदितोक्षणमार्जनादौ । गायन्ति चैनमनुरक्तधियोऽश्रुकण्ठ्यो । धन्या व्रजस्त्रिय उरुक्रमचित्तयान...
english_devnagari: yā dohane'vahanane mathanopalepapreṅkheṅkhanārbharuditokṣaṇamārjanādaugāyanti cainamanuraktadhiyo'śrukaṇṭhyodhanyā vrajastriya urukramacittayānāḥ
translation: Blessed are the women of Vraja who, in the course of their every day activities like milking the kine, pounding corns etc. churning curds for butter, applying cow-dung to the ground, rocking the cradles, lullabying their babies, cleaning and sweeping the houses, glorify him in songs with their hearts deeply attached to him, with their throat choked with tears and who get whatever they wish with their minds absorbed in Lord Kṛṣṇa[3] (or who carry on all their activities while contemplating on Lord Kṛṣṇa).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā urukramacittaṃ tenaiva yānaṃ sarva-viṣaya-prāptir yāsāṃ tāḥ /

devanagari: प्रातर्व्रजाद् व्रजत आविशतश्च सायं । गोभिः समं क्वणयतोऽस्य निशम्य वेणुम् । निर्गम्य तूर्णमबलाः पथि भूरिपुण्याः । पश्यन्ति सस्मितमुखं स...
english_devnagari: prātarvrajād vrajata āviśataśca sāyaṃgobhiḥ samaṃ kvaṇayato'sya niśamya veṇumnirgamya tūrṇamabalāḥ pathi bhūripuṇyāḥpaśyanti sasmitamukhaṃ sadayāvalokam
translation: Inexhaustible must be the fund of merits of those women of Vraja who, coming out on the street quickly on hearing him playing upon the flute, while going out of the Vraja in the morning and returning to Vraja in the evening along with the (herd of) cows, are able to behold his countenance beaming with smiles and radiating grace and compassion.

devanagari: एवं प्रभाषमाणासु स्त्रीषु योगेश्वरो हरिः । शत्रुं हन्तुं मनश्चक्रे भगवान् भरतर्षभ ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ prabhāṣamāṇāsu strīṣu yogeśvaro hariḥśatruṃ hantuṃ manaścakre bhagavān bharatarṣabha
translation: While the women folk were loudly discussing among themselves, the glorious Lord Hari, the Master of Yoga made up his mind to finish with the enemy, Oh Leader of Bharatas!

devanagari: सभयाः स्त्रीगिरः श्रुत्वा पुत्रस्नेहशुचाऽतुरौ । पितरौ अन्वतप्येतां पुत्रयोरबुधौ बलम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sabhayāḥ strīgiraḥ śrutvā putrasnehaśucā'turaupitarau anvatapyetāṃ putrayorabudhau balam
translation: [4] Hearing the panicky discussion of women (full of apprehension), the parents (Vasudeva and Devakī) who were not aware of the (extraordinary) strength of their children, were overwhelmed with filial love and were agitated at heart (full of repentance for not instructing Akrūra not to bring children to Mathura).
purport:
Footnote [4]:
untraced in the text of Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: तैस्तैर्नियुद्धविधिभिः विविधैरच्युतेतरौ । युयुधाते यथान्योन्यं तथैव बलमुष्टिकौ ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: taistairniyuddhavidhibhiḥ vividhairacyutetarauyuyudhāte yathānyonyaṃ tathaiva balamuṣṭikau
translation: Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa) and Cāṇūra fought with each other using diverse manouvers in wrestling. In the same manner did Balarāma wrestle with Muṣṭika.

devanagari: भगवद्‍गात्रनिष्पातैः वज्रनीष्पेषनिष्ठुरैः । चाणूरो भज्यमानाङ्गो मुहुर्ग्लानिमवाप ह ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: bhagavad‍gātraniṣpātaiḥ vajranīṣpeṣaniṣṭhuraiḥcāṇūro bhajyamānāṅgo muhurglānimavāpa ha
translation: It is reported that at every blow and stroke from the Lord’s limbs (such as fisted fore-arms and knees) which were hard like strokes of Vajra (Indra’s thunderbolt), the limbs of Cāṇūra got shattered and he fainted now and then.

devanagari: स श्येनवेग उत्पत्य मुष्टीकृत्य करावुभौ । भगवन्तं वासुदेवं क्रुद्धो वक्षस्यबाधत ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sa śyenavega utpatya muṣṭīkṛtya karāvubhaubhagavantaṃ vāsudevaṃ kruddho vakṣasyabādhata
translation: With the swiftness of a hawk, the enraged Cāṇūra jumped up and clenching both hands into fists, struck the glorious Lord Kṛṣṇa (the son of Vasudeva) on the chest.

devanagari: नाचलत् तत्प्रहारेण मालाहत इव द्विपः । बाह्वोर्निगृह्य चाणूरं बहुशो भ्रामयन् हरिः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: nācalat tatprahāreṇa mālāhata iva dvipaḥbāhvornigṛhya cāṇūraṃ bahuśo bhrāmayan hariḥ
translation: The Lord however did not budge an inch under that blow like an elephant when struck with a wreath of flowers), (on the contrary) catching hold of Cāṇūra by both his arms, he whirled Cāṇūra several times so violently as extinguished his life thereby and dashed him on the ground, where he lay stretched with ornaments and garlands scattered and hair dishevelled, like the flagstaff of Indra struck down.

devanagari: भूपृष्ठे पोथयामास तरसा क्षीण जीवितम् । विस्रस्ताकल्पकेशस्रग् इन्द्रध्वज इवापतत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūpṛṣṭhe pothayāmāsa tarasā kṣīṇa jīvitamvisrastākalpakeśasrag indradhvaja ivāpatat
translation: The Lord however did not budge an inch under that blow like an elephant when struck with a wreath of flowers), (on the contrary) catching hold of Cāṇūra by both his arms, he whirled Cāṇūra several times so violently as extinguished his life thereby and dashed him on the ground, where he lay stretched with ornaments and garlands scattered and hair dishevelled, like the flagstaff of Indra struck down.

devanagari: तथैव मुष्टिकः पूर्वं स्वमुष्ट्याभिहतेन वै । बलभद्रेण बलिना तलेनाभिहतो भृशम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tathaiva muṣṭikaḥ pūrvaṃ svamuṣṭyābhihatena vaibalabhadreṇa balinā talenābhihato bhṛśam
translation: Similarly Muṣṭika was violently slapped with his palms by Balarāma who was dealt a blow with his fist by Muṣṭika.

devanagari: प्रवेपितः स रुधिरं उद्वमन् मुखतोऽर्दितः । व्यसुः पपातोर्व्युपस्थे वाताहत इवाङ्घ्रिपः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: pravepitaḥ sa rudhiraṃ udvaman mukhato'rditaḥvyasuḥ papātorvyupasthe vātāhata ivāṅghripaḥ
translation: Trembling violently (in a swoon) and pressed hard (from within), he vomitted blood profusely through his mouth and fell down dead on the ground, like a tree uprooted by a stormy wind.

devanagari: ततः कूटमनुप्राप्तं रामः प्रहरतां वरः । अवधीत् लीलया राजन् सावज्ञं वाममुष्टिना ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ kūṭamanuprāptaṃ rāmaḥ praharatāṃ varaḥavadhīt līlayā rājan sāvajñaṃ vāmamuṣṭinā
translation: After this, Balarāma, the past master in boxing (lit. in dealing blows) sportively yet scornfully dealt a blow with his left fist and killed another demon Kūṭa who advanced to fight.

devanagari: तर्ह्येव हि शलः कृष्ण प्रपदाहतशीर्षकः । द्विधा विदीर्णस्तोशलक उभावपि निपेततुः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tarhyeva hi śalaḥ kṛṣṇa prapadāhataśīrṣakaḥdvidhā vidīrṇastośalaka ubhāvapi nipetatuḥ
translation: At the same time, Śala (who approached Kṛṣṇa to pull his leg) got his head knocked off by Kṛṣṇa’s kick and the head of Tosalaka (who attempted the same simultaneously) had his head split in two (by Kṛṣṇa’s kick) and both of them fell down lifeless on the ground.

devanagari: चाणूरे मुष्टिके कूटे शले तोशलके हते । शेषाः प्रदुद्रुवुर्मल्लाः सर्वे प्राणपरीप्सवः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: cāṇūre muṣṭike kūṭe śale tośalake hateśeṣāḥ pradudruvurmallāḥ sarve prāṇaparīpsavaḥ
translation: When Cāṇūra, Muṣṭika, Kūṭa, Śala and Tośalaka were thus killed, the rest of the wrestlers, being anxious to save their own lives, ran away.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Out of the various commentaries describing this wrestling, it appears that Subodhinī had a better idea of that art.

devanagari: गोपान् वयस्यान् आकृष्य तैः संसृज्य विजह्रतुः । वाद्यमानेषु तूर्येषु वल्गन्तौ रुतनूपुरौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: gopān vayasyān ākṛṣya taiḥ saṃsṛjya vijahratuḥvādyamāneṣu tūryeṣu valgantau rutanūpurau
translation: Then Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa dragged forth their cowherd-friends in the arena. And clasping them they sported. While the trumpets were flourishing, their anklets too jingled as (Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma) capered and danced.

devanagari: जनाः प्रजहृषुः सर्वे कर्मणा रामकृष्णयोः । ऋते कंसं विप्रमुख्याः साधवः साधु साध्विति ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: janāḥ prajahṛṣuḥ sarve karmaṇā rāmakṛṣṇayoḥṛte kaṃsaṃ vipramukhyāḥ sādhavaḥ sādhu sādhviti
translation: With the exception of Kaṃsa, all spectators were overjoyed at the (athletic) feats of Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa. And all good people including prominent Brāhmaṇas cheered them up with the shouts “Bravo! Well-done!”.

devanagari: हतेषु मल्लवर्येषु विद्रुतेषु च भोजराट् । न्यवारयत् स्वतूर्याणि वाक्यं चेदमुवाच ह ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: hateṣu mallavaryeṣu vidruteṣu ca bhojarāṭnyavārayat svatūryāṇi vākyaṃ cedamuvāca ha
translation: It is traditionally reported that when the stalwart wrestlers, were killed and the rest took to their heals, Kaṃsa, the king of Bhojas, stopped the blowing of all trumpets and commanded as follows:

devanagari: निःसारयत दुर्वृत्तौ वसुदेवात्मजौ पुरात् । धनं हरत गोपानां नन्दं बध्नीत दुर्मतिम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: niḥsārayata durvṛttau vasudevātmajau purātdhanaṃ harata gopānāṃ nandaṃ badhnīta durmatim
translation: “Drive out from the city (Mathura) the two ill- behaved sons of Vasudeva. Confiscate the wealth of all Gopas (cowherds). Arrest and bind down the wicked-minded Nanda.

devanagari: वसुदेवस्तु दुर्मेधा हन्यतामाश्वसत्तमः । उग्रसेनः पिता चापि सानुगः परपक्षगः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: vasudevastu durmedhā hanyatāmāśvasattamaḥugrasenaḥ pitā cāpi sānugaḥ parapakṣagaḥ
translation: As to the evil-minded Vasudeva, the vilest of the vile, let him be put to death immediately along with father Ugrasena and his attendants who have sided with the hostile party.”

devanagari: एवं विकत्थमाने वै कंसे प्रकुपितोऽव्ययः । लघिम्नोत्पत्य तरसा मञ्चमुत्तुङ्गमारुहत् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vikatthamāne vai kaṃse prakupito'vyayaḥlaghimnotpatya tarasā mañcamuttuṅgamāruhat
translation: While Kaṃsa was thus boastfully ordering, the indefatigable Lord Kṛṣṇa flared up in rage and with vehemence he sprang forth with agility to the high dias (occupied by Kaṃsa).

devanagari: तं आविशन्तमालोक्य मृत्युमात्मन आसनात् । मनस्वी सहसोत्थाय जगृहे सोऽसिचर्मणी ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ āviśantamālokya mṛtyumātmana āsanātmanasvī sahasotthāya jagṛhe so'sicarmaṇī
translation: Perceiving Kṛṣṇa so approaching him as his veritable Death, the heroic Kaṃsa instantly sprang from his seat and grasped his sword and shield.

devanagari: तं खड्गपाणिं विचरन्तमाशु । श्येनं यथा दक्षिणसव्यमम्बरे । समग्रहीद् दुर्विषहोग्रतेजा । यथोरगं तार्क्ष्यसुतः प्रसह्य ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ khaḍgapāṇiṃ vicarantamāśuśyenaṃ yathā dakṣiṇasavyamambaresamagrahīd durviṣahogratejāyathoragaṃ tārkṣyasutaḥ prasahya
translation: Like Garuḍa forcibly seizing a serpent, the formidable Lord of irresistible and terrible energy, instantaneously caught hold of Kaṃsa firmly, as he was moving about right and left, like a hawk in the sky.

devanagari: प्रगृह्य केशेषु चलत्किरीतं । निपात्य रङ्गोपरि तुङ्गमञ्चात् । तस्योपरिष्टात् स्वत्स्वयमब्जनाभः । पपात विश्वाश्रय आत्मतन्त्रः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: pragṛhya keśeṣu calatkirītaṃnipātya raṅgopari tuṅgamañcāttasyopariṣṭāt svatsvayamabjanābhaḥpapāta viśvāśraya ātmatantraḥ
translation:
Toppling down his crown and catching Kaṃsa by his hair, the Lord hurled him down from the high dias to the groundfloor of the arena. And on him jumped the absolute willed, (the weighty) support of the (heaviest of the heavy) universe, the veritable Lord Viṣṇu (the lotus-navelled God) himself.
[Vj.’s text adds]
38(A). When Kaṃsa was thus thrown down and crushed by Kṛṣṇa, (the mightily heavy) abode of the three worlds and of absolute will power, Kaṃsa gave up his life in a second (the winking of the eyelid), Oh King.

devanagari: तं सम्परेतं विचकर्ष भूमौ । हरिर्यथेभं जगतो विपश्यतः । हा हेति शब्दः सुमहान् तदाभूद् । उदीरितः सर्वजनैर्नरेन्द्र ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ samparetaṃ vicakarṣa bhūmauhariryathebhaṃ jagato vipaśyataḥhā heti śabdaḥ sumahān tadābhūdudīritaḥ sarvajanairnarendra
translation: And like unto a lion dragging an elephant, Lord Hari dragged him on the ground though lifeless, even while the world was witnessing it (aghast). Loud shrieks, screams and shouts expressing grief and horror burst forth from all the people, Oh King.

devanagari: स नित्यदोद्विग्नधिया तमीश्वरं । पिबन् वदन् वा विचरन् स्वपन्श्वसन् । ददर्श चक्रायुधमग्रतो यतः । तदेव रूपं दुरवापमाप ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sa nityadodvignadhiyā tamīśvaraṃpiban vadan vā vicaran svapanśvasandadarśa cakrāyudhamagrato yataḥtadeva rūpaṃ duravāpamāpa
translation: With a mind agitated with fright, he always visualized in front of him the Lord armed with a discus even while he was drinking, speaking, wandering, sleeping (nay even) breathing. Hence after death, he attained (identity with) that very form which is so very difficult (for others) to attain.

devanagari: तस्यानुजा भ्रातरोऽष्टौ कंकन्यग्रोधकादयः । अभ्यधावन् अतिक्रुद्धा भ्रातुर्निर्वेशकारिणः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: tasyānujā bhrātaro'ṣṭau kaṃkanyagrodhakādayaḥabhyadhāvan atikruddhā bhrāturnirveśakāriṇaḥ
translation: Kaṅka, Nyagrodhaka, and other eight younger brothers of Kaṃsa became enraged and attacked him with a view to repay the moral debt of their brother by avenging his death.

devanagari: तथातिरभसांस्तांस्तु संयत्तात् रोहिणीसुतः । अहन् परिघमुद्यम्य पशूनिव मृगाधिपः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: tathātirabhasāṃstāṃstu saṃyattāt rohiṇīsutaḥahan parighamudyamya paśūniva mṛgādhipaḥ
translation: With the tusk of the elephant as a club (parigha) Balarāma, the son of Rohiṇī, struck down and finished all those who rushed fully prepared and with vehement impetuosity, as a lion kills beasts.

devanagari: नेदुर्दुन्दुभयो व्योम्नि ब्रह्मेशाद्या विभूतयः । पुष्पैः किरन्तस्तं प्रीताः शशंसुर्ननृतुः स्त्रियः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: nedurdundubhayo vyomni brahmeśādyā vibhūtayaḥpuṣpaiḥ kirantastaṃ prītāḥ śaśaṃsurnanṛtuḥ striyaḥ
translation: Kettle-drums were sounded in the heavens. Great divinities like Brahmā, Śiva and others were highly delighted and sang songs of glory, while showering flowers on him. And celestial women danced.

devanagari: तेषां स्त्रियो महाराज सुहृन्मरणदुःखिताः । तत्राभीयुर्विनिघ्नन्त्यः शीर्षाण्यश्रुविलोचनाः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ striyo mahārāja suhṛnmaraṇaduḥkhitāḥtatrābhīyurvinighnantyaḥ śīrṣāṇyaśruvilocanāḥ
translation: The widowed women, however, who were deeply grieved at the death of their husbands and friends came there beating their heads and eyes overflowing with tears, Oh great king.

devanagari: शयानान् न्वीरशय्यायां पतीन् आलिङ्ग्य शोचतीः । विलेपुः सुस्वरं नार्यो विसृजन्त्यो मुहुः शुचः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: śayānān nvīraśayyāyāṃ patīn āliṅgya śocatīḥvilepuḥ susvaraṃ nāryo visṛjantyo muhuḥ śucaḥ
translation: Embracing their husbands lying dead on the ground the grieving ladies lamented loudly, shedding frequently profuse tears.

devanagari: हा नाथ प्रिय धर्मज्ञ करुणानाथवत्सल । त्वया हतेन निहता वयं ते सगृहप्रजाः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: hā nātha priya dharmajña karuṇānāthavatsalatvayā hatena nihatā vayaṃ te sagṛhaprajāḥ
translation: (They bewailed: “Ah! My Lord! My beloved, knower of dharma! Oh merciful Lord! Oh affectionate darling! By your death we, along with your home and progeny are as good as killed.

devanagari: त्वया विरहिता पत्या पुरीयं पुरुषर्षभ । न शोभते वयमिव निवृत्तोत्सवमङ्गला ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tvayā virahitā patyā purīyaṃ puruṣarṣabhana śobhate vayamiva nivṛttotsavamaṅgalā
translation: Oh foremost among men! Bereaved of you the protector, the city of Mathura does not appear beautiful as it is bereft of joyousness, festivals and auspiciousness.

devanagari: अनागसां त्वं भूतानां कृतवान् द्रोहमुल्बणम् । तेनेमां भो दशां नीतो भूतध्रुक् को लभेत शम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: anāgasāṃ tvaṃ bhūtānāṃ kṛtavān drohamulbaṇamtenemāṃ bho daśāṃ nīto bhūtadhruk ko labheta śam
translation: Oh Lord!You perpetrated utmostcruelty and harshness towards the innocents. Hence, you have been brought to such a plight. What oppressors of living beings can get happiness?

devanagari: सर्वेषामिह भूतानां एष हि प्रभवाप्ययः । गोप्ता च तदवध्यायी न क्वचित् सुखमेधते ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: sarveṣāmiha bhūtānāṃ eṣa hi prabhavāpyayaḥgoptā ca tadavadhyāyī na kvacit sukhamedhate
translation: For, this Kṛṣṇa is verily the beginning (creator), end (destroyer) and the protector of all beings. He who is insubordinate to him shall never get any happiness.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । राजयोषित आश्वास्य भगवान् लोकभावनः । यामाहुर्लौकिकीं संस्थां हतानां समकारयत् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācarājayoṣita āśvāsya bhagavān lokabhāvanaḥyāmāhurlaukikīṃ saṃsthāṃ hatānāṃ samakārayat
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having consoled the ladies of the royal family, the glorious Lord who is the protector of the world, caused what is known as the formal obsequies and funeral rites of those who were killed.

devanagari: मातरं पितरं चैव मोचयित्वाथ बन्धनात् । कृष्णरामौ ववन्दाते शिरसा स्पृश्य पादयोः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: mātaraṃ pitaraṃ caiva mocayitvātha bandhanātkṛṣṇarāmau vavandāte śirasā spṛśya pādayoḥ
translation: Thereupon, Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma secured the release of their father and mother from their chains and touching their feet with their heads, paid respects to them.

devanagari: देवकी वसुदेवश्च विज्ञाय जगदीश्वरौ । कृतसंवन्दनौ पुत्रौ सस्वजाते न शङ्कितौ ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: devakī vasudevaśca vijñāya jagadīśvaraukṛtasaṃvandanau putrau sasvajāte na śaṅkitau
translation:
Realizing that their sons who were bowing down are verily the Lords of the world, Devakī and Vasudeva being afraid (of a possible offence to them) did not embrace them.
[back to top]
